last_completed_round.direction_name: Restore Exact Round-69 Base And Recheck Export Lifetime From There
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 71 changed PTX issue grouping inside the 64x64 compute helpers by flattening the recursive right-left column issue into an explicit Step 0/1/2/3 sequence, then remeasured 25.7464323 ms. That improved relative to round 70 but stayed slower than the exact round-69 base at 25.49913597 ms, so this explicit PTX helper issue-grouping variant is not a win on the exact base and should be closed negative. With grouped_rows sweeps, snake launch-order locality, fixed-K peeling, export traversal changes, A-first refill, consumer-order sweeps, broad stage overexpansions, and the narrow handoff-closure family already closed, the best remaining bounded family on the active default path is a low-rank export-lifetime recheck from the exact round-69 base. This keeps grouped_rows=8, the accepted right-left 64x64 consume order, reversed row-pair traversal, linear export traversal, one-sync steady-state handoff, B-first refill, and accepted helper shapes fixed, and only asks whether a carefully re-scoped export-lifetime edit can move runtime from the true reproducible base rather than from a drifted branch.
